_metadata.title: FEMA P-58 casualty consequence parameters (Philippine school portfolio)
_metadata.description: Injury + fatality consequence parameters for the casualty decision variables (the thesis's 2nd and 3rd DVs, beyond loss + downtime). Two pathways per FEMA P-58 (2018a) and Jeswani (2021) Ch 6.2.6.2 / Ch 7.4.2-7.4.3: (a) non-collapse component-driven injuries via the affected-area model; (b) collapse-driven fatalities + injuries via HAZUS-typology collapse casualty rates. Demolition (irreparable, non-collapse) produces NO additional casualties beyond its component damage states (thesis Ch 6; demolition is a financial/recovery total-loss state, not a life-safety event).
_metadata.source_thesis: Jeswani, K. K. (2021), MASc thesis, University of Toronto. Ch 6.2.2 (casualty consequences), Ch 6.2.6.2 (population + collapse casualty), Tables D-1/D-2 (DS casualty summaries, p.296-297), Ch 7.4.2/7.4.3 (portfolio injuries/fatalities).
_metadata.model: casualties = (a) non-collapse: sum over components of [n_damaged_units_at_casualty_DS * affected_area_sf_per_unit * population_density * casualty_rate_theta]; population_density = population / total_floor_area_sf. (b) collapse: collapse_realization * population * P_total_collapse * {fatality: collapse_fatality_rate; injury: 1 - collapse_fatality_rate}. The two pathways are summed (a collapsed building's component injuries are subsumed by the collapse term; in this implementation non-collapse component injuries are only counted on NON-collapsed realizations to avoid double counting).

units.affected_area_sf: square feet affected per damaged P-58 unit
units.rate: fraction of occupants in the affected area who become a casualty of that severity
units.population_density: occupants per square foot of total floor area

_metadata.generated_by: OPUS casualty agent 2026-06-27 (manual extraction from component_fragilities.yaml + archetype_fragilities.yaml + Ch6/Ch7). Data-driven: code reads this file, never hardcodes values.
_metadata.provenance_overall: high for non-collapse component injury thetas/areas (Tables D-1/D-2, directly tabulated); medium for collapse_fatality_rate_by_hazus_type (thesis states 'Default FEMA P-58 (2018a) mean collapse fatality rates based on HAZUS typology' but does not tabulate the exact per-type numbers in the public thesis; values below are the standard published Hazus-MH EQ Technical Manual Severity-4 indoor casualty rates by model building type, NOT fitted to the thesis outputs).

population_basis.note: Per-building occupant count used as casualty exposure. The thesis normalizes injuries/fatalities by the default P-58 generated population (Table 6-5) but the .mat anchor (Bldg_CasI/Bldg_CasF) scales each building's casualties by its ACTUAL population (MCQC inventory). We use the inventory 'population' field (actual students), consistent with the .mat reconstruction (implied exposure 560,133 ~ actual MC+QC students 560,283).

noncollapse_component_casualties._comment: Non-collapse injury/fatality consequences keyed by PH component ID. Each entry lists the damage state(s) that carry a casualty consequence and the affected area per damaged P-58 unit. Source: thesis Tables D-1/D-2 (printed p.296-297) via docs/thesis/data/component_fragilities.yaml. Structural RC/PT/steel components carry NO non-collapse casualties (thesis Ch 6.2.2). unit_sf is the component's P-58 quantity-unit size in SF (for EA components unit_sf=null: damaged quantity is already a unit count).

PH.NS.CHB.SU.label: CHB Wall; Solid; Unreinforced
PH.NS.CHB.SU.note: Thesis: CHB casualty consequences approximated, subject to further review (Table D-4 footnote).

PH.NS.CHB.PU.label: CHB with Doors/Windows; Unreinforced
PH.NS.CHB.PU.note: CHB casualty consequences approximated, subject to further review.

PH.NS.FIX.NS.label: Ceiling Fixtures; Non-Seismic
PH.NS.FIX.NS.note: Seismically-rated fixtures (PH.NS.FIX.SE) carry NO casualty (thesis Ch 6.2.2).

PH.NS.ELEC.WM.label: Wall-Mounted Electronics
PH.NS.ELEC.WM.note: Only non-structural component with a non-collapse FATALITY rate (thesis Ch 6.4.2); high demand capacity (PFA fragility median 2.5 g) means it rarely fires.

collapse_casualties._comment: Collapse-driven casualties (FEMA P-58 2018a). fatalities = collapse * population * P_total_collapse * collapse_fatality_rate * expected_occupancy_factor; collapse injuries = collapse * population * P_total_collapse * (1 - collapse_fatality_rate) * expected_occupancy_factor. The (1 - P_total_collapse) partial-collapse-mode share is treated as NO additional casualty (conservative w.r.t. fatalities; consistent with the thesis emphasis that fatalities derive from TOTAL collapse). collapse_fatality_rate is the lethal-area rate by HAZUS model building type (thesis Ch 6.2.6.2: 'Default FEMA P-58 (2018a) mean collapse fatality rates based on HAZUS typology adopted; Collapse injury rate = 1 minus the fatality rate'). The expected_occupancy_factor (FEMA P-58 population model, see expected_occupancy_factor_by_archetype) scales the collapse exposure to the population expected to be PRESENT at the time of the earthquake. As of the NC-injury recalibration (2026-06-27) it is applied to BOTH the fatality and injury terms (expected_occupancy_factor_applies_to = 'both'): collapse injuries, like collapse fatalities, accrue only to present occupants. Applying it to fatalities ONLY left the collapse-injury exposure at full enrolment, which made collapse injuries DOMINATE the total and inverted the thesis's non-collapse-dominant injury split (0.78-0.99 Makati). See docs/learnings/2026-06-27_casualties.md.

expected_occupancy_factor_by_archetype._comment: FEMA P-58 (2018a) population/occupancy model: the casualty exposure at the instant of collapse is the population EXPECTED TO BE PRESENT, not the full enrolled population. The thesis applied 'the P-58 population model for middle schools to all archetypes' (Ch 6.2.6.2) and reports both an 'Actual Population' and a much smaller 'P-58 Peak Population' per archetype (Table 6-5, printed p.131). This factor = (P-58 Peak Population / Actual Population) per archetype; it is the FEMA P-58 expected-present-occupancy fraction that PACT applied to the collapse fatality computation. Applying it to the fatality term (only) reconciles the whole-portfolio WVF-7.3 fatalities to the thesis .mat anchor (PA_CasF mean 2945) within ~15%, WITHOUT moving injuries. Without it, fatalities run ~7x high (full-enrollment exposure). Provenance: thesis Table 6-5 actual-vs-P-58-peak population columns; high confidence on the tabulated populations, the ratio interpretation is the FEMA P-58 expected-occupancy mechanism.

expected_occupancy_factor_provenance.source: Thesis Jeswani (2021) Table 6-5 (printed p.131): per-archetype 'Actual Population | P-58 Peak Population'. Factor = P-58 Peak / Actual. Values: C1-M (Hi) 156/481; C1-M (Mid) 141/810; C1-M (Pre/Lo) 141/846; C1-L (Mid/Hi) 92/256; C1-L (Pre/Lo) 141/272; C1-H (Hi) 2534/1687; PTC1-M (Hi) 416/1109; PTC1-M (Mid) 165/407; S1-M (Hi) 428/2453; S3-L 17/53; CHB-L 16/51; W-L 56/152; CWS-L 92/219; N-L 22/94. C1-M (Pre/Lo) FRP reuses C1-M (Pre/Lo). Population-weighted mean ~0.295 over the real inventory.
expected_occupancy_factor_provenance.mechanism: FEMA P-58 (2018a) Vol 1 expected-present-occupancy: collapse casualties (BOTH fatalities and injuries) are computed on the population present at the time of the earthquake (a fraction of peak/enrolled occupancy). The thesis reports the P-58 generated 'peak' population which it used to normalize casualties (Ch 7.4 / Table 6-5); PACT applied this occupancy basis to the collapse fatality AND injury calculation. C1-H (Hi) factor exceeds 1 because its P-58-generated peak population for a large multi-storey building exceeds the recorded enrolment; this raises its (small, 2-building, low-collapse) collapse contribution negligibly.

C1L.note: Low/mid-rise concrete moment frame; Hazus-MH EQ Tech Manual Severity-4 collapse indoor casualty rate.

C1H.note: High-rise concrete; Hazus uses ~0.15 for the collapsed portion.

W1.note: Wood/light frame; lower collapse fatality rate.

URM.note: Unreinforced masonry (CHB); highest collapse fatality rate.

collapse_casualties.provenance_source: P_total_collapse from thesis Table 6-6 (p.133) via docs/thesis/data/archetype_fragilities.yaml; collapse_fatality_rate_by_hazus_type = standard Hazus-MH EQ Technical Manual Severity-4 indoor casualty rates (medium confidence; the exact per-type numbers are not tabulated in the public thesis).

noncollapse_injury_calibration._comment: Per-archetype calibration that raises the FEMA P-58 affected-area non-collapse INJURY output to the thesis's published per-archetype non-collapse injury rate. The affected-area model (component affected areas 16-250 SF + injury theta ~0.10 over the building-average occupant density population/total_floor_area) systematically UNDER-COUNTS non-collapse injuries relative to the thesis: our baseline whole-portfolio NC injuries are ~6,000 vs the thesis ~40,000 (the thesis reports per-archetype injury rates 0.017-0.17 vs our affected-area 0.002-0.025). Without this, even after the expected-occupancy factor is applied to collapse injuries, the non-collapse injury fraction stays ~0.27 (collapse-injury-dominated) instead of the thesis's non-collapse-dominant split. factor_by_archetype scales the summed, component-resolved NC injury term per archetype so the modelled per-archetype NC injury rate matches the thesis. Applied to NC INJURIES ONLY (applies_to='injuries_only'); NC fatalities (wall-mounted electronics/elevator) are negligible and already match. Because the factor multiplies the component-resolved NC sum uniformly, the non-structural mitigation's component-level neutralisation is preserved (the reduction stays proportional).

provenance.source: Thesis Jeswani (2021) per-archetype injuries; decoded from the thesis portfolio-analysis .mat (sandbox/thesis-data/Portfolio_Analysis_Script/WVF_7_3_PA.mat, Arch_simp_norm_CasI = per-archetype injury rate / actual population, Arch_simp_norm_CasF = per-archetype fatality rate).
provenance.derivation: factor[a] = thesis_nc_injury_rate[a] / baseline_affected_area_nc_rate[a]. thesis_nc_injury_rate[a] = Arch_simp_norm_CasI[a] - min((1-CFR)/CFR * Arch_simp_norm_CasF[a], Arch_simp_norm_CasI[a]) — i.e. the thesis per-archetype TOTAL injury rate minus the collapse-injury portion implied by the thesis's own stated collapse model (collapse injury rate = 1 - fatality rate, thesis Ch 6.2.6.2). baseline_affected_area_nc_rate[a] = our un-calibrated affected-area NC injuries per enrolled occupant, from the WVF-7.3 base calibration run (N=1000, seed 12345).
provenance.provenance_confidence: high for the thesis per-archetype injury rates (the thesis's own published output); medium for the per-archetype factor (it is anchored to the WVF-7.3 base run and inherits the .mat collapse-injury decomposition).
provenance.validation: With this calibration + expected_occupancy_factor_applies_to='both', the WVF-7.3 base reproduces the thesis injury structure: whole-portfolio injuries ~59,000 (.mat PA_CasI median 58,117) and non-collapse injury fraction ~0.73 whole / ~0.76 Makati / ~0.74 QC — matching the thesis's own .mat-reconstructed fractions (whole 0.70, Makati 0.79 = the Ch 7.4.2 '0.78-0.99' Makati anchor, QC 0.68). The earlier model (no occupancy factor on collapse injuries, no NC calibration) gave a collapse-dominated fraction ~0.11.
provenance.caveat: The factors are large (and largest for the high-collapse low-rise archetypes N-L, S3-L, W-L and the Pre/Lo RCMRF) for two compounding, documented reasons: (1) the affected-area reading genuinely under-counts NC injuries ~2-17x even for low-collapse archetypes; (2) for the high-collapse archetypes our collapse rate exceeds the 2021-thesis per-archetype collapse rate (the documented upstream collapse-fragility-distribution difference, out of scope here), which suppresses our non-collapsed NC sample and inflates the factor needed to reach the thesis per-archetype NC LEVEL. A single global factor ~6.5x reproduces the WHOLE anchors (injuries ~56k, NC fraction ~0.71) but not the regional structure; the per-archetype factors are used because the task target is the thesis's per-archetype rates. CHB-L is set to 1.0 (its thesis non-collapse injury rate is ~0 — collapse-dominated URM — so no raise; <3 injuries portfolio-wide). C1-M (Pre/Lo) FRP reuses C1-M (Pre/Lo).

derivation_detail._comment: Per-archetype intermediate quantities behind factor_by_archetype, for full traceability. normI/normF from WVF_7_3_PA.mat; thesis_nc_injury_rate per the derivation above; baseline_nc_injury_rate from the WVF-7.3 N=1000 seed-12345 base calibration run.

CHB-L.note: thesis NC rate ~0 (collapse-dominated); factor floored to 1.0
